decisions.question: Where does gate type schema information live for compile-time validation? The compiler (template/types.rs) needs to know each gate type's output field names and value types. gate.rs already imports from template/types.rs, so types.rs cannot import from gate.rs without creating a circular dependency. Options include: (A) a static GateTypeSchema registry in template/types.rs alongside existing GATE_TYPE_* constants, (B) derive schema at compile time from built_in_default() output keys/types (requires moving that function to types.rs or a shared module), (C) inline schema knowledge directly in the validate() method without a shared abstraction.

What should override_default validation enforce? When a gate declares override_default, the compiler should validate it against the gate type's schema. Options: (A) exact match - all schema fields must be present, no extra fields, types must be compatible; (B) subset match - present fields must have correct types, no extra fields allowed, missing fields OK; (C) type-check only - rejects wrong types but not missing or extra fields; (D) warn only - compile succeeds but emits warnings for type mismatches.

How deep should gates.* when clause validation go? The compiler already splits when clause fields into gates.* and agent fields, but doesn't validate gates.* references. Options: (A) gate existence only - verify gate_name in gates.<gate_name>.<field> names an actual gate declared in the state; (B) gate + field existence - also verify field_name is a valid field in the gate type's schema; (C) gate + field + value type - additionally verify the comparison value's type is compatible with the schema field type. Also decide: should an incorrectly structured path like 'gates.foo' (too short) or 'gates.foo.bar.baz' (too deep) be an error or silently ignored?

How should the reachability check be scoped and implemented? PRD R9 requires: when override defaults are applied to all gates, at least one transition resolves. Options for scope: (A) only check states where all conditional transitions use exclusively gates.* fields (pure-gate states) - avoids false positives on mixed gate+agent states; (B) check all gated states, but require at least one pure-gate transition to satisfy; (C) skip the reachability check entirely, treating it as best-effort per PRD. Options for implementation: (i) inline resolver call - build override evidence map and call the existing resolve_transition logic at compile time; (ii) pattern analysis - inspect when conditions analytically without running resolver; (iii) error vs. warning for dead ends.
